title: Star Wars Screen Entertainment

'''''Star Wars'' Screen Entertainment''' was a product of [[LucasArts]] Entertainment Company and Presage Software Development, Inc.,<ref name="Adv 7" /> released in [[June]] [[1994]].<ref name="Date" /> It was a 3.5″ diskette, (and later as a CD-ROM as part of the [[The LucasArts Archives Vol. I]] collection), including different [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']]–themed screen savers. It was was available for Windows operating system 3.1 or higher, as well as for Macintosh system 7.0 and above.<ref name="Adv 7" />

Screen Entertainment does not include any interactive game. However, the user can customize some aspects of the screen savers (number of stars, speed, etc.). The icon to execute the program was [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], which was also used in the [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] option.

Early promotional materials referred to the software as the '''''Star Wars Screen Saver'''''.<ref name="Adv 7" />

==Contents==
===Screen savers===
*'''Blueprints:''' Blueprints of ships including details, plus indications of the location of each of its parts (e.g., [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] in the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]).
*'''Cantina:''' A static photo of the [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Mos Eisley Cantina]], then the system chooses one of the characters in the cantina and explains his or her story.
*'''Character biographies:''' Profiles focusing on the lead characters of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']]. Most of the details center on that film's plot.
*'''Darth Vader:''' The message "The Dark Lord of the Sith approaches" followed by a static close-up of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], adding voice and/or breathing.
*'''Death Star Trench:''' An animation of the trench, at high speed. Users can choose if they want only an [[X-wing starfighter|X-wing]], only a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]], or both (in this case, the X-wing will be chasing the TIE).
*'''Hyperspace:''' A semi-static picture of the ''Millennium Falcon'' cockpit, with moving stars outside. Suddenly, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] moves his arm and the stars become lines as if entering the [[hyperspace]]. After a while, Han moves again and the lines become stars. [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] periodically howls.
*'''Imperial Clock:''' The "clock" used by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Tarkin]] while reaching Yavin 4, adding a real 24- or 12-hour digital clock.
[[File:JawaInvasion.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Jawas invading Wookieepedia]]
*'''Jawas:''' Small Jawas, [[droid]]s, [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]], [[Womp rat/Legends|womp rats]] and [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] move across the screen. The Jawas sometimes "open" the screen to see if there's anything of value under it. Tusken Raiders scare the Jawas, who stop whatever they were doing and run away.
*'''Lightsaber Duel:''' Similar to Jawas, it features small Darth Vader attacking small [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]].
*'''Poster Art:''' It features different posters from ''A New Hope''.
*'''Rebel Clock:''' Similar to Imperial clock, with [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Dodonna's]] display of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. It is analog. The [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] marks minutes and Yavin 4 marks hours. The Death Star might be over Yavin.
*'''Scrolling Text:''' Recreates the film's [[opening crawl]]. User can edit the crawl and write his or her own text. It also included a hidden message from [[George Lucas]] about his plans to release the [[prequel trilogy]].
*'''Space Battles:''' As seen from some window in a cruiser, small dots move against each other and shoot beams. [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari cruisers]], [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigates]] and [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] drift slowly across the screen while fighters occasionally fly by.
*'''Storyboards:''' Real storyboards from ''A New Hope'', alongside the script.

==Continuity==
Some of the information in the biographies became outdated after the release of the [[List of changes in Star Wars re-releases|1997 re-release]] and the [[Prequel trilogy|prequels]]. It identifies [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]] as Obi-Wan Kenobi's brother (which originated in the [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Return of the Jedi'' novelization]] and has since been contradicted), and is the only source listing [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Lars']] maiden name as Beru Weth (later revealed to be Beru Whitesun). It also includes alternate names for some Mos Eisley Cantina patrons, many later revealed to be aliases in other sources.

The software's biography of Darth Vader includes details of his past that are very similar to the plot of the prequels, still years away. This is also the original source of Grand Moff Tarkin's first name, which has since become [[canon]]; it was initially spelled "Willhuf" in the software, though later spelled as "Wilhuff" in later media.

In the Jawas and Lightsaber Duel options, the images are from the video game ''[[Super Star Wars]]''.
